Grafton of Labor in any legislative
program that jointly affected the
welfare of the farmers and the
wage earners.


Mrs. May Arkwright Hutton,
II. A. Jarvis and Mrs. Rose Moore,
representing the recent suffrage
organization, were present to
thank the farmers for the support
In the recent election.


Resolutions were adopted de-
handing that Prosecutor-elect
Wiley complete the probe into the
county affairs begun by the last
grand jury; condemning the state
aid highway law, and asking for
it's repeal; demanding that the
new legislative apportionment give
the farmers four representatives
and two senators located outside
the city of Spokane; demanding
larger powers of local self govern
ment through amendments to the
township laws.
